---
title: "Theorem 5.7 — Distribution of s² and independence of β̂"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic_hasLaw_chiSquared"
chapter: 5
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Forward-looking references

The Lean theorems referenced below are **aspirational**. As of this writing,
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean` is only ~107 lines long and
contains exactly one of the named objects below:
`olsResidualVarianceEstimator_linear_model_quadratic_form` (currently at line
56, not line 69 as cited in the original walkthrough).

The remaining objects — `scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic`,
`scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic_hasLaw_chiSquared`,
`olsBeta_indep_scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic`,
`residual_quadratic_form_eq_sum_sq_eigenvector_coords`, and
`scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic_eq_sum_sq_eigenvector_coords` — are
planned but not yet formalized. The line numbers cited in the "Lean statement"
and "Lean proof structure" sections below should be updated once those
theorems land.
:::

## Textbook statement

> Paraphrased textbook statement: in the homoskedastic normal regression
> model,
>
> $$
> \frac{(n-k)s^2}{\sigma^2} \sim \chi^2_{n-k}
> \qquad \text{and} \qquad
> \hat\beta \perp s^2.
> $$

## Assumptions

- Linear model: $y = X\beta + e$ where $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k}$ has full column rank.
- Homoskedastic Gaussian errors: $e \sim N(0, \sigma^2 I_n)$.
- $n > k$ (so degrees of freedom $n-k$ is positive and $s^2$ is well-defined).

## Lean statement

Main objects and theorems:

- [`scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L30) at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean:30`
- [`scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic_hasLaw_chiSquared`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L270) at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean:270`
- [`olsBeta_indep_scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L355) at `HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean:355`

The Lean statistic is the scaled random variable

$$
\omega \mapsto \frac{(n-k)s^2(\omega)}{\sigma^2}.
$$

So the first theorem is literally the chi-square law Hansen states.

The independence theorem is stated using the scaled statistic rather than raw
$s^2$. This is equivalent, because the map

$$
s^2 \mapsto \frac{n-k}{\sigma^2}s^2
$$

is a deterministic positive rescaling.

## Translation notes

The only presentation difference is the scaling of $s^2$ in the independence
statement. Mathematically, this is harmless and exactly equivalent to
Hansen's claim: independence of $\hat\beta$ and any deterministic, injective
function of $s^2$ is equivalent to independence of $\hat\beta$ and $s^2$
itself.

So the Lean theorem is proving the right theorem, not just a nearby one.

## Proof sketch

Let

$$
M = I - X(X'X)^{-1}X'.
$$

Then under the linear model,

$$
\hat e = My = M(X\beta + e) = Me,
$$

so

$$
(n-k)s^2 = \hat e'\hat e = e'Me.
$$

Since $M$ is symmetric and idempotent, it has an orthonormal eigenbasis with
eigenvalues in $\{0,1\}$. Therefore

$$
e'Me = \sum_{\lambda_i = 1} (b_i' e)^2.
$$

After dividing by $\sigma^2$,

$$
\frac{(n-k)s^2}{\sigma^2} = \sum_{\lambda_i = 1} \left(\frac{b_i' e}{\sqrt{\sigma^2}}\right)^2.
$$

Because $e \sim N(0, \sigma^2 I)$, the standardized rotated coordinates are
independent $N(0,1)$ variables. The number of nonzero terms is

$$
\operatorname{rank}(M) = n-k.
$$

Hence

$$
\frac{(n-k)s^2}{\sigma^2} \sim \chi^2_{n-k}.
$$

For independence, write

$$
\hat\beta - \beta = Ae,
\qquad
\hat e = Me,
\qquad
A = (X'X)^{-1}X'.
$$

These are jointly Gaussian linear images of $e$. Their cross-covariance is

$$
\operatorname{Cov}(Ae, Me)
= A(\sigma^2 I)M'
= \sigma^2 AM'
= \sigma^2 (X'X)^{-1} X' M
= 0.
$$

So $\hat\beta$ is independent of the residual-based variance statistic.

## Lean proof structure

The key internal steps in Lean are:

- [`olsResidualVarianceEstimator_linear_model_quadratic_form`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L69)
- [`residual_quadratic_form_eq_sum_sq_eigenvector_coords`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L122)
- [`scaledOlsResidualVarianceStatistic_eq_sum_sq_eigenvector_coords`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter5NormalRegression.lean#L193)
- [`orthonormalBasis_coords_div_sqrt_iIndep_standardGaussian`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/ProbabilityUtils.lean)
- [`hasLaw_sum_sq_chiSquared_fintype`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/ChiSquared.lean)

For independence, the proof then uses the Gaussian linear-image route rather
than spectral decomposition:

- centered coefficient error is a linear image of the Gaussian error vector
- residual vector is another linear image
- their cross-covariance is zero because $X' M = 0$
- jointly Gaussian plus zero cross-covariance implies independence

## Downstream uses

- (To be filled in once Hansen Theorem 5.7 dependents are formalized.)
